content: First-line: lifestyle modification - weight loss target 7-10% improves steatosis, ≥10% may reverse fibrosis; Mediterranean diet preferred; aerobic and resistance exercise beneficial independent of weight loss. Vitamin E (alpha-tocopherol 800 IU/day) recommended for non-diabetic adults with biopsy-proven NASH (PIVENS trial); potential prostate cancer/hemorrhagic stroke risk requires discussion. Pioglitazone improves NASH histology in diabetics and non-diabetics (weight gain side effect). GLP-1 receptor agonists (liraglutide, semaglutide) show promise for NASH resolution in trials. Obeticholic acid (FXR agonist) approved 2023 for NASH with fibrosis. Bariatric surgery effective for eligible patients - resolves NASH in ~85%, improves fibrosis. Manage cardiovascular risk factors aggressively (statins safe in NAFLD). Avoid hepatotoxic substances. Coffee consumption associated with reduced fibrosis. No FDA-approved pharmacotherapy specifically for NAFLD until recently; several agents in phase 3 trials.
